model.label: Claude Opus 4.6
model.description: Anthropic • Hybrid reasoning model that pushes the frontier for coding and AI agents, featuring a 1M context window.

pick.rationale: Purdue's historically elite offense—ranked #1-2 nationally with an AdjO of 131-132.6—creates a roughly 10-point efficiency margin advantage over Miami (FL) (+30.5 vs +20.7), a gap that is wider than the typical 2-vs-7 matchup and should be decisive despite Purdue's well-documented defensive vulnerabilities (AdjD ~100.5, 36th-45th nationally). The Boilermakers enter with tremendous momentum after winning four games in four days to capture the Big Ten Tournament championship, including an 80-72 victory over #2-ranked Michigan, while Miami exits the ACC Tournament on a sour note after an 84-62 blowout loss to Virginia. Miami's solid but unspectacular offense (AdjO 121.4, 34th nationally) lacks the firepower to exploit Purdue's defensive weaknesses at a level that would close the massive overall quality gap, making Purdue a comfortable favorite to advance.

reasoningStep.title: Purdue's elite offense and Big Ten Tournament momentum overwhelm Miami (FL)
reasoningStep.summary: Purdue holds a commanding ~10-point adjusted efficiency margin advantage driven by the nation's top-ranked offense, and enters riding a Big Ten Tournament championship run, while Miami's average offense cannot exploit Purdue's defensive weaknesses enough to close the gap.
evidence: Purdue KenPom #8 (AdjEM +30.5, AdjO 131.0 #2, AdjD 100.5); Miami KenPom #30 (AdjEM +20.67, AdjO 121.4 #34, AdjD 100.7)
Purdue Torvik #8 (AdjOE 132.6 #1, AdjDE 100.2 #36); Miami Torvik #35 (AdjOE 121.1 #35, AdjDE 101.5 #45)
Purdue won Big Ten Tournament championship, beating Michigan 80-72 in the final after 4 games in 4 days
Miami lost to Virginia 84-62 in ACC Tournament, entering NCAAs on a poor note
Purdue's defense was a known concern (lost 97-93 to Wisconsin in regular season finale) but team responded with dominant BTT run
2-seeds historically beat 7-seeds approximately 85% of the time; this matchup features a wider-than-typical quality gap

result.text: Purdue basketball wins Big Ten Tournament championship over Michigan Skip to main content Home Olympics NFL NCAAB NCAAW NBA Women's Sports NCAAF MLB WNBA Sports Betting Big Ten Sports SEC Sports Boxing Coaches Salaries Draft Hub Fantasy Football For The Win Formula 1 Golfweek High School Sports High School Sports Awards IndyCar MMA NASCAR National Signing Day NHL Soccer Sports Business Sports Pulse Sports Seriously Tennis WWE Youth Sports Outdoors Fantasy Bet With the People Clear Play Arizona Sports Indiana Sports Michigan Sports Ohio Sports Tennessee Sports Wisconsin Teams For Subscribers Oscar Cluff inspired Purdue basketball stuns Michigan in Big Ten title game Sam King Lafayette Journal & Courier Show Caption Hide Caption Purdue basketball coach Matt Painter on UCLA win in Big Ten Tournament Hear what Matt Painter said about Purdue basketball advancing to the Big Ten Tournament championship game. CHICAGO — Four games in four days, culminating with a Big Ten Tournament championship game against perhaps the best team in the country. Purdue basketball was up for the challenge. Oscar Cluff's dunk with 1:17 to go put a stamp on the Boilermakers ' 80-72 title game victory over regular-season Big Ten champion Michigan on Sunday, March 15 at the United Center. Cluff sealed the title win at the free-throw line late, along with four straight makes from Jack Benter. It's the third Big Ten Tournament championship for Purdue, which won in 2009 and 2023, the latter also at United Center. Purdue basketball turning point vs Michigan in Big Ten title game After Elliott Cadeau's basket just before the halftime buzzer tied the score at 38, Purdue responded with a second-half haymaker out of the gate. More: Why Purdue basketball guard Braden Smith is wearing a 41 jersey vs Michigan The Boilermakers scored seven straight points, ignited by Fletcher Loyer's 3-pointer, to open the half. Braden Smith and Trey Kaufman-Renn went to the pick and roll Michigan had no answer for, resulting in four straight made baskets for Kaufman-Renn. Smith's conventional three-point play with 12:06 to go put the Boilermakers ahead 58-46. How many assists does Braden Smith need to break NCAA record? Braden Smith entered Sunday's game against Michigan needing 12 assists to tie Bobby Hurley's NCAA record of 1,076. Smith finished with 11 assists and is now one shy of Hurley's mark. 3 Purdue basketball standouts vs. Michigan in Big Ten title game Trey Kaufman-Renn: Big Ten Defensive Player of the Year Aday Mara met his match in Purdue's fifth-year post. Kaufman-Renn had 20 points on 10-of-15 shooting. Braden Smith: Smith was 0-for-3 before changing out of his ripped No. 3 jersey in exchange for 41. In a new number, Smith went 6 for 9 and scored 14 points to complete a double-double. Oscar Cluff: Purdue's center led the way to Purdue getting to the Big Ten championship game. He capped his impressive week with a 21-point, five-rebound performance. Sam King covers sports for the Journal & Co

result.text: Purdue basketball defense questioned after loss vs Wisconsin. Can it be fixed? Skip to main content Home NCAAB NCAAW MLB Women's Sports NFL NBA NCAAF WNBA Sports Betting Big Ten Sports SEC Sports Boxing Coaches Salaries Draft Hub Fantasy Football For The Win Formula 1 Golfweek High School Sports High School Sports Awards IndyCar MMA NASCAR National Signing Day NHL Olympics Soccer Sports Business Sports Pulse Sports Seriously Tennis WWE Youth Sports Outdoors Fantasy Bet With the People Clear Play Arizona Sports Indiana Sports Michigan Sports Ohio Sports Tennessee Sports Wisconsin Teams For Subscribers How Wisconsin became the latest to expose Purdue basketball's defense, yet to answer its issues Nathan Baird Indianapolis Star Show Caption Hide Caption Purdue basketball coach Matt Painter Wisconsin postgame Hear what Matt Painter said after Purdue basketball’s senior day loss to Wisconsin. Purdue men's basketball lost its regular-season finale to Wisconsin, 97-93, on senior day. Coach Matt Painter and players acknowledged the team's defensive struggles are a primary concern. Wisconsin became the fourth Big Ten opponent to defeat Purdue at Mackey Arena this season. WEST LAFAYETTE — Purdue men’s basketball celebrated the legacy of a transformative senior class Saturday in Mackey Arena. Boilermakers coach Matt Painter extolled the virtues of each one during the program’s customary senior day festivities following the regular-season finale. Before thanking those seniors for everything they have accomplished, Painter addressed the uncertain future. “Our goals are still right there in front of us, so we’ve got to keep going,” Painter said following a 97-93 loss to Wisconsin . “And it's not easy being great. it is not easy. But I promise you this, speaking for these guys, speaking for our staff, we absolutely won’t give in to this shit. We’re going to keep fighting.” Purdue has waged some form of that battle all season. The conflict between the team it believes it can be and the one which keeps showing up began with a home blowout loss to Iowa State on Dec. 6. It continued with a home shootout loss to the Badgers on Saturday. The Boilermakers now must decide what this specific team’s legacy will be. Will it find enough defensive consistency which, combined with its offensive proficiency, could make it as dangerous as any March Madness entrant? Or, does Saturday’s loss and the home losses which preceded it suggest Purdue, regardless of how many points it scores, may always be one stop away from the kind of victories necessary to make a deep run? “We just need to lock in and be better defensively,” Trey Kaufman-Renn said. “... We have complete confidence in ourselves offensively. We’ve proven that. But defensively, we’ve just got to be better.” Wisconsin became the fourth Big Ten opponent to win at Mackey. Three of them scored 88 or more points. Iowa State scored 81 back in December, too, but could take its foot off the gas late. Twice on Saturday, when the

result.text: Purdue basketball wins Big Ten Tournament championship over Michigan Skip to main content Home Olympics NFL NCAAB NCAAW NBA Women's Sports NCAAF MLB WNBA Sports Betting Big Ten Sports SEC Sports Boxing Coaches Salaries Draft Hub Fantasy Football For The Win Formula 1 Golfweek High School Sports High School Sports Awards IndyCar MMA NASCAR National Signing Day NHL Soccer Sports Business Sports Pulse Sports Seriously Tennis WWE Youth Sports Outdoors Fantasy Bet With the People Clear Play Arizona Sports Indiana Sports Michigan Sports Ohio Sports Tennessee Sports Wisconsin Teams For Subscribers Oscar Cluff inspired Purdue basketball stuns Michigan in Big Ten title game Sam King Lafayette Journal & Courier Show Caption Hide Caption Purdue basketball coach Matt Painter on UCLA win in Big Ten Tournament Hear what Matt Painter said about Purdue basketball advancing to the Big Ten Tournament championship game. CHICAGO — Four games in four days, culminating with a Big Ten Tournament championship game against perhaps the best team in the country. Purdue basketball was up for the challenge. Oscar Cluff's dunk with 1:17 to go put a stamp on the Boilermakers ' 80-72 title game victory over regular-season Big Ten champion Michigan on Sunday, March 15 at the United Center. Cluff sealed the title win at the free-throw line late, along with four straight makes from Jack Benter. It's the third Big Ten Tournament championship for Purdue, which won in 2009 and 2023, the latter also at United Center. Purdue basketball turning point vs Michigan in Big Ten title game After Elliott Cadeau's basket just before the halftime buzzer tied the score at 38, Purdue responded with a second-half haymaker out of the gate. More: Why Purdue basketball guard Braden Smith is wearing a 41 jersey vs Michigan The Boilermakers scored seven straight points, ignited by Fletcher Loyer's 3-pointer, to open the half. Braden Smith and Trey Kaufman-Renn went to the pick and roll Michigan had no answer for, resulting in four straight made baskets for Kaufman-Renn. Smith's conventional three-point play with 12:06 to go put the Boilermakers ahead 58-46. How many assists does Braden Smith need to break NCAA record? Braden Smith entered Sunday's game against Michigan needing 12 assists to tie Bobby Hurley's NCAA record of 1,076. Smith finished with 11 assists and is now one shy of Hurley's mark. 3 Purdue basketball standouts vs. Michigan in Big Ten title game Trey Kaufman-Renn: Big Ten Defensive Player of the Year Aday Mara met his match in Purdue's fifth-year post. Kaufman-Renn had 20 points on 10-of-15 shooting. Braden Smith: Smith was 0-for-3 before changing out of his ripped No. 3 jersey in exchange for 41. In a new number, Smith went 6 for 9 and scored 14 points to complete a double-double. Oscar Cluff: Purdue's center led the way to Purdue getting to the Big Ten championship game. He capped his impressive week with a 21-point, five-rebound performance. Sam King covers sports for the Journal & Co

Skip to main content Home NCAAB NCAAW MLB Women's Sports NFL NBA NCAAF WNBA Sports Betting Big Ten Sports SEC Sports Boxing Coaches Salaries Draft Hub Fantasy Football For The Win Formula 1 Golfweek High School Sports High School Sports Awards IndyCar MMA NASCAR National Signing Day NHL Olympics Soccer Sports Business Sports Pulse Sports Seriously Tennis WWE Youth Sports Outdoors Fantasy Bet With the People Clear Play Arizona Sports Indiana Sports Michigan Sports Ohio Sports Tennessee Sports Wisconsin Teams For Subscribers How Wisconsin became the latest to expose Purdue basketball's defense, yet to answer its issues Nathan Baird Indianapolis Star Show Caption Hide Caption Purdue basketball coach Matt Painter Wisconsin postgame Hear what Matt Painter said after Purdue basketball’s senior day loss to Wisconsin. Purdue men's basketball lost its regular-season finale to Wisconsin, 97-93, on senior day. Coach Matt Painter and players acknowledged the team's defensive struggles are a primary concern. Wisconsin became the fourth Big Ten opponent to defeat Purdue at Mackey Arena this season. WEST LAFAYETTE — Purdue men’s basketball celebrated the legacy of a transformative senior class Saturday in Mackey Arena. Boilermakers coach Matt Painter extolled the virtues of each one during the program’s customary senior day festivities following the regular-season finale. Before thanking those seniors for everything they have accomplished, Painter addressed the uncertain future. “Our goals are still right there in front of us, so we’ve got to keep going,” Painter said following a 97-93 loss to Wisconsin . “And it's not easy being great. it is not easy. But I promise you this, speaking for these guys, speaking for our staff, we absolutely won’t give in to this shit. We’re going to keep fighting.” Purdue has waged some form of that battle all season. The conflict between the team it believes it can be and the one which keeps showing up began with a home blowout loss to Iowa State on Dec. 6. It continued with a home shootout loss to the Badgers on Saturday. The Boilermakers now must decide what this specific team’s legacy will be. Will it find enough defensive consistency which, combined with its offensive proficiency, could make it as dangerous as any March Madness entrant? Or, does Saturday’s loss and the home losses which preceded it suggest Purdue, regardless of how many points it scores, may always be one stop away from the kind of victories necessary to make a deep run? “We just need to lock in and be better defensively,” Trey Kaufman-Renn said. “... We have complete confidence in ourselves offensively. We’ve proven that. But defensively, we’ve just got to be better.” Wisconsin became the fourth Big Ten opponent to win at Mackey. Three of them scored 88 or more points. Iowa State scored 81 back in December, too, but could take its foot off the gas late. Twice on Saturday, when the
